input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check what's available
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install latest .NET 8 SDK (or 9, adjust as needed)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target requires WinUI/WinAppSDK)

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --silent `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
              --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.MSIX.Packaging `
              --includeRecommended --quiet --wait"
```

## 4. Install Java JDK (Android build dependency)

```powershell
# MAUI installs OpenJDK automatically via workload, but to verify:
dotnet workload list

# If you need it manually:
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --silent
```

## 5. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload auto-provisions the Android SDK, but to inspect/manage:

```powershell
# Default location after workload install:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"

# List installed packages
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Discover available platform versions
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "platforms;"

# Install a specific API level (e.g., 34)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "platforms;android-34"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 6. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk", "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\jdk\jdk-17*", "User")

# Refresh current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
```

## 7. Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows deployment)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1"
```

## 8. Create & Verify a Project

```powershell
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# List available target frameworks in the project
Select-String '<TargetFrameworks>' *.csproj
```

## 9. Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
  Select-String "system-images"

# Install an image + create AVD
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"
```

---

**Key version-discovery commands to remember:**

| What | Command |
|------|---------|
| .NET SDKs available | `winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK` |
| Installed SDKs | `dotnet --list-sdks` |
| MAUI workloads | `dotnet workload search maui` |
| Android APIs | `sdkmanager.bat --list` |
| Target frameworks | `dotnet new maui --dry-run` or check `.csproj` |

Run these steps top-to-bottom in an elevated PowerShell and you'll have a fully working MAUI environment for both targets.
